---
title: "Facilities & Capabilities"
description: "OptoLAB experimental capabilities and representative laboratory equipment."
canonical-url: "https://optolab-unimore.github.io/facilities.html"
---

OptoLAB supports the development of complete optical measurement chains, from experimental layouts and optomechanics to electronic acquisition, calibration and computational analysis.

::: {.card-grid .three}
::: {.topic-card}
### Optical prototyping

Experimental layouts for illumination, collection, imaging and spectral sensing, supported by C-mount optics, broadband mirrors, filters and optomechanical components.
:::

::: {.topic-card}
### Imaging sensors

Machine-vision cameras for experimental image acquisition, including Matrix Vision/Balluff BlueFOX3 USB3 camera systems in 3 MP and 5 MP configurations.
:::

::: {.topic-card}
### Electronic instrumentation

Sensor interfaces, acquisition electronics and optoelectronic system integration for weak-signal and multi-channel measurements.
:::

::: {.topic-card}
### Embedded platforms

Compact computing and embedded-acquisition systems for device control, communication, portable instrumentation and edge processing.
:::

::: {.topic-card}
### Calibration workflows

Methods and software for sensor characterization, reference comparison, photometric and radiometric calibration and model fitting.
:::

::: {.topic-card}
### Computational prototyping

Dedicated computing resources and in-house additive manufacturing for data analysis, instrument housings, optical supports and test parts.
:::
:::

## Representative equipment

The current laboratory records include:

- Matrix Vision/Balluff BlueFOX3 USB3 machine-vision cameras, including a 5 MP BF3-5M-0051ZG-110510 system;
- C-mount lenses and mounts, broadband mirrors for 750–1100 nm, a hot mirror and related optical components;
- LattePanda Alpha compact development systems used in portable ophthalmic image-acquisition prototypes;
- a dedicated computing workstation for modelling, acquisition and analysis;
- a Creality CR-10 Smart Pro 3D printer for rapid prototyping.

This is a representative capability overview rather than a complete booking inventory. Configurations, availability and access conditions should be confirmed with the laboratory.
